other_observers: Initially just me. Later Bill Schmoker, Michael Freiberg and others;
description: Brown Aythya duck with blotchy gray and brown sides. Vent white. Sides separated from vent by dark brown vertical strap. Back solid blackish brown. Chest mottled brown, darker than sides, but not dramatically so. Head showed tuft consistent with adult female (but could 1st fall male show a tuft of this length?). Head dark glossy brown, with no whitish, including around base of bill. Bill blue-gray with black tip and distinct white subterminal band. Bill shape narrower than that of scaup, typical of Tufted DuckBehaviors: Mostly sleeping, occasionally preeningCall: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
